executive_summary: Strong week with actionable infrastructure across Markets and Payments. Six high-urgency trading tools emerged—Lean and Nautilus Trader both support options backtesting critical for your NIFTY CE/PE first-candle strategy. Hyperswitch offers immediate cost reduction potential for Redpin's multi-processor routing. One AI efficiency pick (persistent planning for n8n) rounds out the signal. No direct Reel IQ picks this week, though OpenBB's API normalization pattern has cross-domain relevance.

top_picks.headline: Backtest NIFTY options strategy with production-grade engine
top_picks.why_now: Your first-5-minute-candle CE/PE thesis needs rigorous validation before capital deployment; Lean's options support and Indian market data connectors make it the fastest path to statistical confidence.
top_picks.action: Clone Lean, configure NSE data feed, and backtest 90 days of first-candle entries on NIFTY weekly options by Friday.

top_picks.headline: Cut Redpin processor fees with intelligent routing
top_picks.why_now: Multi-processor orchestration directly attacks your Chase/Cross River cost basis; production-ready with active enterprise deployments.
top_picks.action: Schedule 30-min discovery call with Juspay team this week to scope Redpin integration timeline and fee impact.

top_picks.headline: Rust-native backtesting for latency-sensitive options
top_picks.why_now: If Lean's Python overhead concerns you for intraday options, Nautilus provides microsecond-precision event replay—critical for validating first-candle entries where timing is everything.
top_picks.action: Run parallel backtest on same 90-day dataset as Lean; compare fill assumptions and execution latency modeling.

top_picks.headline: Unified data layer for NIFTY derivatives research
top_picks.why_now: Consolidates fragmented market data sources into single API—eliminates manual data wrangling eating your research time.
top_picks.action: Install OpenBB SDK, test NSE derivatives data coverage, replace ad-hoc yfinance scripts if depth is sufficient.

top_picks.headline: Crash-proof your complex n8n automations
top_picks.why_now: Your multi-step automations fail silently mid-execution; persistent markdown planning with completion gates adds observability and recovery.
top_picks.action: Fork repo, prototype wrapper around one high-value n8n workflow, validate state persistence across restarts.

top_picks.headline: Curated quant toolkit for strategy expansion
top_picks.why_now: 26K-star aggregation of backtesting frameworks, indicators, and anomaly detection—saves weeks of tool discovery for both trading and potential Redpin fraud detection crossover.
top_picks.action: Audit options-specific tools section, bookmark 3 libraries for post-backtest strategy iteration.

skip_list.reason: Educational resource—valuable for upskilling but not actionable this week when you need backtesting infrastructure, not coursework.
Already likely in your stack; OpenBB supersedes for derivatives depth and API consolidation.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Maintenance notice — June 2026**
> Due to funding constraints, the public [Fincept Terminal](https://github.com/Fincept-Corporation/FinceptTerminal) is moving to **one update per month** and is **no longer maintained on a daily basis**. The team is now focused on a subscription-based **private edition** of the terminal and a new project, **[Quantcept](https://github.com/Fincept-Corporation/Quantcept)**. This open-source repo stays public and will not be deleted.
